questions.name: Majority Element
questions.statement: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element. The majority element is the element that appears more than n/2 times. The majority element always exists.
questions.hint: Boyer-Moore Voting: maintain a candidate and count; count resets to 0 when it drops below zero.
questions.solution: Initialize candidate=nums[0], count=1. For each element: if count==0 set candidate=element. If element==candidate increment count, else decrement. Return candidate.

questions.name: Move Zeroes
questions.statement: Given an integer array nums, move all 0s to the end while maintaining the relative order of non-zero elements. Do it in-place.
questions.hint: Use a slow pointer insertPos for the next non-zero position.
questions.solution: Keep insertPos=0. Iterate with i: when nums[i]!=0, place it at nums[insertPos] and advance insertPos. Fill positions from insertPos to end with 0.

questions.name: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
questions.statement: Given an array of integers and a number k, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size k.
questions.hint: Compute sum of first k elements, then slide window: add next element, remove first element.
questions.solution: Compute windowSum = sum of first k elements. Set maxSum=windowSum. For i=k to n-1: windowSum += arr[i] - arr[i-k]. Update maxSum. Return maxSum.

questions.name: Longest Subarray with Sum K
questions.statement: Given an array of integers (possibly negative) and integer k, return the length of the longest subarray whose sum equals k.
questions.hint: Use prefix sum + hash map storing first occurrence index of each prefix sum.
questions.solution: Maintain prefixSum and a map of {prefixSum: firstIndex}. For each i, if prefixSum-k exists in map, update maxLen = max(maxLen, i - map[prefixSum-k]). If prefixSum not in map, store it.

questions.name: First Negative in Every Window of Size K
questions.statement: Given an array and positive integer k, find the first negative integer for each window of size k. Print 0 if no negative exists in a window.
questions.hint: Use a deque to store indices of negative numbers within the current window.
questions.solution: Use a deque of indices of negatives. For each i: remove front if out of window, add i if arr[i]<0. Append arr[deque.front] or 0 to result when window size reaches k.

questions.name: Running Sum of 1D Array
questions.statement: Given an array nums, return an array where runningSum[i] = sum of nums[0] through nums[i].
questions.hint: For each i from 1 to n-1, add previous element to current.
questions.solution: Iterate from index 1 to n-1: nums[i] += nums[i-1]. Return modified nums.

questions.name: Equilibrium Index
questions.statement: Find an index in an array such that the sum of elements to its left equals the sum to its right. Return -1 if no such index exists.
questions.hint: totalSum - leftSum - arr[i] = rightSum. Return i when leftSum equals rightSum.
questions.solution: Compute totalSum. Iterate maintaining leftSum. At each index if leftSum == totalSum - leftSum - arr[i], return index. Add arr[i] to leftSum. Return -1.

questions.name: Rotate Array
questions.statement: Given an integer array nums, rotate the array to the right by k steps.
questions.hint: Reverse the whole array, then reverse first k, then reverse remaining n-k.
questions.solution: k = k % n. Reverse entire array. Reverse first k elements. Reverse remaining n-k elements.

questions.name: Leaders in an Array
questions.statement: Given an array, find all leaders. An element is a leader if it is greater than all elements to its right. The rightmost element is always a leader.
questions.hint: Traverse from right maintaining maxRight. If arr[i] > maxRight, it's a leader.
questions.solution: Last element is always leader. Traverse right to left with maxRight. If arr[i] > maxRight, add to result and update maxRight. Reverse result.

questions.name: First Unique Character in a String
questions.statement: Given a string s, find the first non-repeating character and return its index. If it does not exist, return -1.
questions.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^5
s consists of only lowercase English letters.
questions.hint: Count frequencies using array of 26. Return index of first char with count 1.
questions.solution: Count frequency of each character using array[26]. Iterate string again, return index of first character with frequency == 1.

questions.name: Reverse String
questions.statement: Write a function that reverses an array of characters in-place using O(1) extra space.
questions.hint: Use two pointers from both ends, swap until they meet in the middle.
questions.solution: Initialize l=0, r=n-1. While l < r: swap s[l] and s[r], l++, r--.

questions.name: Reverse Words in a String
questions.statement: Given an input string s, reverse the order of the words. Remove leading/trailing spaces and reduce multiple spaces to single space.
questions.hint: Trim, split by whitespace, reverse the array, join with single space.
questions.solution: Trim string. Split by one or more whitespace characters. Reverse the words array. Join with single space.

questions.name: Longest Common Prefix
questions.statement: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings. Return empty string if no common prefix exists.
questions.hint: Compare characters vertically across all strings. Stop at first mismatch.
questions.solution: Use first string as reference. For each character position, check if all strings have same char. Stop when mismatch or any string ends. Return prefix found.

questions.name: Roman to Integer
questions.statement: Given a roman numeral string s, convert it to an integer.
questions.hint: If current value < next value, subtract current; otherwise add current.
questions.solution: Map Roman symbols to values. Traverse string. If value[i] < value[i+1], subtract value[i] from total. Otherwise add value[i].

questions.name: String Compression
questions.statement: Compress a character array in-place: consecutive repeating characters are replaced by the character followed by its count (omit count if 1). Return new length.
questions.hint: Use read and write pointers. Count consecutive chars, write char then count digits.
questions.solution: Read pointer i scans, write pointer w writes. Count consecutive same chars. Write char at w. If count>1, write each digit of count. Return w.

questions.name: Check String Rotation
questions.statement: Given two strings s and goal, return true if s can become goal after some number of left rotations.
questions.hint: s is a rotation of goal if and only if goal is a substring of s+s.
questions.solution: If lengths differ return false. Return whether goal is a substring of (s+s). Use indexOf or KMP.

questions.name: Character Frequency Count
questions.statement: Given a string, find and return the frequency of each character present in the string.
questions.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^4
s consists of lowercase letters.
questions.hint: Use an array of 26 integers or a HashMap to count occurrences.
questions.solution: Create freq[26] initialized to 0. For each char c in s: freq[c-'a']++. Return map of char to its frequency for all non-zero entries.

questions.name: Search Insert Position
questions.statement: Given a sorted array of distinct integers and a target, return the index if found. If not, return the index where it would be inserted in order.
questions.hint: Standard binary search; when not found, the left pointer is the insertion position.
questions.solution: Binary search with l=0, r=n-1. When target found return mid. When l>r, return l (insertion point).

questions.name: First and Last Position of Element
questions.statement: Given a sorted array and target, find the starting and ending position of target. If not found, return [-1, -1].
questions.hint: Run binary search twice: once biased left for first occurrence, once biased right for last.
questions.solution: First: binary search, when found continue searching left (r=mid-1). Last: binary search, when found continue searching right (l=mid+1). Return both positions.

questions.name: Find Peak Element
questions.statement: A peak element is strictly greater than its neighbors. Given an array, find any peak element index. Adjacent elements are not equal.
questions.hint: If nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], peak must be to the right; else to the left.
questions.solution: Binary search: if nums[mid] < nums[mid+1], set l=mid+1 else r=mid. Loop while l<r. Return l.

questions.name: Square Root using Binary Search
questions.statement: Given a non-negative integer x, return the floor of its square root without using sqrt().
questions.hint: Binary search from 1 to x. Find largest mid where mid*mid <= x.
questions.solution: If x<2 return x. Binary search l=1, r=x/2. If mid*mid==x return mid. If mid*mid<x, ans=mid, l=mid+1. Else r=mid-1. Return ans.

questions.name: Detect Cycle Start in Linked List
questions.statement: Given the head of a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null.
questions.constraints:
0 <= number of nodes <= 10^4
pos is -1 or a valid index
questions.hint: After slow and fast meet inside cycle, reset one pointer to head; move both one step at a time.
questions.solution: Floyd's: detect meeting point. Reset slow to head. Move slow and fast one step at a time. Where they meet again is the cycle entry node.

questions.name: Middle of Linked List
questions.statement: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node. If two middle nodes, return the second middle.
questions.hint: Slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2 steps; when fast reaches end, slow is at middle.
questions.solution: slow=head, fast=head. While fast!=null and fast.next!=null: slow=slow.next, fast=fast.next.next. Return slow.

questions.name: Palindrome Linked List
questions.statement: Given the head of a singly linked list, return true if it is a palindrome.
questions.hint: Find middle, reverse the second half, compare both halves.
questions.solution: Find middle with slow/fast pointers. Reverse second half from middle.next. Compare first and reversed second half node by node.

questions.name: Add Two Numbers
questions.statement: Two non-empty linked lists represent two non-negative integers stored in reverse order. Add the two numbers and return sum as a linked list.
questions.hint: Simulate digit-by-digit addition with carry using a dummy head node.
questions.solution: Use dummy head. Add digits plus carry: sum=val1+val2+carry. New node = sum%10, carry=sum/10. Handle remaining nodes. Add extra node if carry remains.

questions.name: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
questions.statement: Given heads of two singly linked lists, return the node where they intersect. Return null if they don't intersect.
questions.hint: Two pointers each traverse both lists; they meet at intersection after m+n steps.
questions.solution: a=headA, b=headB. Advance both. When a==null redirect to headB; when b==null redirect to headA. They meet at intersection or both null.

questions.name: Min Stack
questions.statement: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
questions.hint: Maintain two stacks: main stack and a min-tracking stack.
questions.solution: Main stack stores all values. Min stack stores minimum at each level. On push: push to both (min stack gets min of new val and current min). On pop: pop from both.

questions.name: Next Greater Element
questions.statement: For each element in nums1 (subset of nums2), find the next greater element to its right in nums2. Return -1 if none exists.
questions.constraints:
1 <= nums1.length <= nums2.length <= 1000
All integers are unique
questions.hint: Use monotonic decreasing stack on nums2 to map each element to its next greater.
questions.solution: Process nums2 with monotonic stack. When larger element found, pop stack and map popped→current. For nums1 elements, return map value or -1.

questions.name: Stock Span Problem
questions.statement: The span of a stock price today is the maximum number of consecutive days (including today) the price was less than or equal to today's price. Return span for each day.
questions.hint: Stack stores (price, span) pairs. Pop while top price <= current, accumulate spans.
questions.solution: For each price: span=1. While stack not empty and top.price <= current price: span += top.span, pop. Push (price, span). Return span.

questions.name: First Non-Repeating Character in Stream
questions.statement: Given a stream of characters, at each point print the first non-repeating character. Print '#' if no such character exists.
questions.hint: Use frequency array and a queue of candidates. Pop front when its frequency > 1.
questions.solution: freq[26] array + queue. For each char: increment freq, enqueue char. While queue front has freq>1, dequeue. Print front or '#' if empty.

questions.name: Search in a BST
questions.statement: Given root of a BST and an integer val, find the node whose value equals val and return the subtree rooted at it. Return null if not found.
questions.hint: Use BST property: if val < root go left, if val > root go right.
questions.solution: If root==null or root.val==val return root. If val < root.val recurse left else recurse right.

questions.name: Insert into a BST
questions.statement: Given root of a BST and a value to insert (guaranteed not to exist), return the root after insertion.
questions.constraints:
0 <= node count <= 10^4
All values are unique
questions.hint: Traverse using BST property; insert new node at the null leaf position.
questions.solution: If root==null create and return new node. If val < root.val recurse left. Else recurse right. Return root.

questions.name: Factorial using Recursion
questions.statement: Given a non-negative integer n, return its factorial using recursion. factorial(0)=1, factorial(n)=n*factorial(n-1).
questions.hint: Base case n<=1 returns 1. Recursive case returns n * factorial(n-1).
questions.solution: if(n<=1) return 1; return n * factorial(n-1);

questions.name: Sum of Digits using Recursion
questions.statement: Given a non-negative integer n, find the sum of its digits using recursion.
questions.hint: sumDigits(n) = n%10 + sumDigits(n/10). Base case: n==0 returns 0.
questions.solution: if(n==0) return 0; return (n%10) + sumDigits(n/10);

questions.name: Power Function (Fast Exponentiation)
questions.statement: Implement pow(x, n) which calculates x raised to the power n. Handle negative exponents and n=0.
questions.hint: If n is even: pow(x,n)=pow(x,n/2)². If n is odd: x*pow(x,n-1). Handle negative n.
questions.solution: if n<0 return pow(1/x,-n). if n==0 return 1. if n%2==0: half=pow(x,n/2); return half*half. else return x*pow(x,n-1).

questions.name: Tower of Hanoi
questions.statement: Move n disks from source to destination using auxiliary rod. A larger disk cannot be placed on a smaller disk. Print all moves.
questions.sampleOutput:
Move disk 1 from A to C
Move disk 2 from A to B
Move disk 1 from C to B
questions.hint: Move n-1 disks to aux, move nth to dest, move n-1 from aux to dest.
questions.solution: hanoi(n,src,dest,aux): if n==1 print move. Else: hanoi(n-1,src,aux,dest), print move disk n, hanoi(n-1,aux,dest,src).

questions.name: N-Queens
questions.statement: Place n queens on an n×n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Return all distinct solutions.
questions.hint: Track attacked columns and diagonals using sets. Place one queen per row.
questions.solution: Backtrack row by row. Use sets for cols, left-diags (row-col), right-diags (row+col). Try each column, recurse for next row. Add solution when all rows placed.

questions.name: Jump Game II
questions.statement: Given an array of non-negative integers where each element is max jump length, return the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index.
questions.hint: Track current jump end and farthest reachable. Jump when reaching current end.
questions.solution: jumps=0, currentEnd=0, farthest=0. For each i (except last): farthest=max(farthest,i+nums[i]). If i==currentEnd: jumps++, currentEnd=farthest. Return jumps.

questions.name: Assign Cookies
questions.statement: Given greed factors g[] and cookie sizes s[], maximize the number of content children. A child is content if cookie size >= greed factor.
questions.hint: Sort both arrays. Use two pointers: if cookie >= greed, satisfy child and advance both.
questions.solution: Sort g and s. i=0 (children), j=0 (cookies). While both valid: if s[j]>=g[i] increment i (satisfied). Always increment j. Return i.

questions.name: Non-Overlapping Intervals
questions.statement: Given an array of intervals, return the minimum number of intervals to remove to make the rest non-overlapping.
questions.hint: Sort by end time. Greedily keep intervals with earliest end. Count removed.
questions.solution: Sort by end. prevEnd=intervals[0][1], count=0. For i=1 to n-1: if start>=prevEnd update prevEnd. Else count++ (remove current, keep smaller end). Return count.

questions.name: Activity Selection Problem
questions.statement: Given n activities with start and end times, select the maximum number of non-overlapping activities that can be performed by a single person.
questions.hint: Sort by end time. Greedily select activity if start >= lastEnd.
questions.solution: Sort activities by end time. Select first, count=1, lastEnd=end[0]. For each i>0: if start[i]>=lastEnd, count++, lastEnd=end[i]. Return count.

questions.name: Job Sequencing Problem
questions.statement: Given jobs with deadlines and profits, find the sequence of jobs that maximizes total profit. Each job takes 1 unit of time and must be done before its deadline.
questions.hint: Sort by profit descending. For each job, find latest free slot before deadline.
questions.solution: Sort by profit desc. Boolean slots array. For each job: find latest free slot from min(deadline,maxD) down to 1. Schedule if slot found.

questions.name: Partition Equal Subset Sum
questions.statement: Given integer array nums, return true if you can partition it into two subsets with equal sum.
questions.hint: If total sum is odd return false. Find subset with sum = total/2 using 0/1 knapsack.
questions.solution: If totalSum odd return false. target=totalSum/2. dp[target+1] boolean, dp[0]=true. For each num: update dp backwards dp[j]|=dp[j-num]. Return dp[target].

questions.name: Merge K Sorted Lists
questions.statement: Given an array of k sorted linked-lists, merge all into one sorted linked-list and return it.
questions.hint: Use a min-heap of size k. Always extract minimum and push its next node.
questions.solution: Add head of each non-null list to min-heap. Extract min, append to result, push its next to heap. Repeat until heap empty.

questions.name: Binary Tree Zigzag Level Order Traversal
questions.statement: Given root of a binary tree, return zigzag level order traversal: left to right, then right to left, alternating.
questions.hint: BFS level order; toggle a direction flag per level and reverse accordingly.
questions.solution: BFS with queue. For each level collect values. If level index is odd, reverse the values array before adding to result.

questions.name: Word Ladder
questions.statement: Find the length of shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord where each step changes exactly one letter and each intermediate word must be in wordList. Return 0 if no path exists.
questions.hint: BFS from beginWord. At each step try all single-char substitutions and check wordSet.
questions.solution: BFS: start with beginWord at distance 1. For each word, try changing each position to a-z. If result in wordSet, enqueue with distance+1 and remove from set. Return distance at endWord.

questions.name: Course Schedule II
questions.statement: Given numCourses and prerequisites, return an ordering of courses to finish all courses. If impossible (cycle), return empty array.
questions.hint: Kahn's algorithm: BFS from all 0-indegree nodes. Return order or empty if cycle.
questions.solution: Build adj list + indegree array. Queue all 0-indegree nodes. Process: add to order, decrement neighbors' indegrees, enqueue those reaching 0. Return order if size==n else [].

questions.name: Design Add and Search Words
questions.statement: Design a data structure for addWord and search, where search supports '.' as wildcard matching any single letter.
questions.constraints:
1 <= word.length <= 25
word consists of lowercase letters or '.'
questions.hint: Standard trie for addWord. For search, use DFS with '.' matching any child.
questions.solution: Build trie normally. search uses DFS: at '.', try all 26 children recursively. At regular char, follow that child. Return true if any path reaches end with isEnd=true.

questions.name: Check Prime Number
questions.statement: Given a positive integer n, determine if it is prime. A prime number has no divisors other than 1 and itself.
questions.hint: Check divisibility from 2 to √n. If any divides n evenly, not prime.
questions.solution: If n<2 return false. If n==2 return true. If n%2==0 return false. Check odd i from 3 to √n: if n%i==0 return false. Return true.

questions.name: Armstrong Number
questions.statement: Check if a number is an Armstrong number (narcissistic number): sum of digits each raised to the power of number of digits equals the number itself. E.g. 153 = 1³+5³+3³.
questions.hint: Count digits d, sum each digit^d, compare with original.
questions.solution: Count digits d. Extract each digit, compute digit^d, accumulate sum. Return sum == original n.

questions.name: Perfect Number
questions.statement: A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (excluding itself). Given n, return true if it is a perfect number.
questions.hint: Sum divisors up to √n. For each divisor i, add both i and n/i (if distinct from i and n).
questions.solution: If n<=1 return false. sum=1. For i=2 to √n: if n%i==0, add i; if i!=n/i add n/i. Return sum==n.

questions.name: Strong Number
questions.statement: A Strong Number has sum of factorials of its digits equal to the number itself. E.g. 145 = 1! + 4! + 5! = 1+24+120.
questions.hint: Precompute factorials 0-9. Extract each digit, sum its factorial. Compare with n.
questions.solution: Precompute fact[0..9]. Sum fact[digit] for each digit of n. Return sum == n.

questions.name: Palindrome Number
questions.statement: Given an integer x, return true if x reads the same forwards and backwards. Negative numbers are not palindromes.
questions.hint: Negative numbers and those ending in 0 (except 0) are not palindromes. Reverse half the digits.
questions.solution: If x<0 or (x%10==0 && x!=0) return false. Reverse half: while x>reversed: reversed=reversed*10+x%10, x/=10. Return x==reversed or x==reversed/10.

questions.name: Count Digits of a Number
questions.statement: Given a non-negative integer n, count the number of digits in it.
questions.hint: Divide n by 10 repeatedly until 0, counting iterations.
questions.solution: If n==0 return 1. count=0. While n>0: n=n/10, count++. Return count. Or return (int)Math.log10(n)+1.

questions.name: Reverse Integer
questions.statement: Given a signed 32-bit integer x, return x with its digits reversed. Return 0 if reversing causes overflow.
questions.hint: Extract digits via modulo. Check overflow before each multiplication.
questions.solution: result=0. While x!=0: digit=x%10, x=x/10. Before result=result*10+digit, check if it would overflow INT_MAX/MIN. Return result.

questions.name: Counting Bits
questions.statement: Given an integer n, return array ans of length n+1 where ans[i] is the number of 1s in the binary representation of i.
questions.hint: dp[i] = dp[i>>1] + (i&1). Even numbers have same bit count as i/2.
questions.solution: dp[0]=0. For i=1 to n: dp[i] = dp[i>>1] + (i & 1). Return dp.

questions.name: Power of Two
questions.statement: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two.
questions.hint: A power of two has exactly one set bit. n & (n-1) == 0 for powers of 2.
questions.solution: If n<=0 return false. Return (n & (n-1)) == 0.

questions.name: Set Matrix Zeroes
questions.statement: Given an m×n matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0. Do it in-place.
questions.hint: Use first row and first column as markers. Handle them separately.
questions.solution: Check if row0 or col0 has zero. Use matrix[i][0] and matrix[0][j] as zero markers. Zero inner cells. Apply row markers, col markers. Handle row0 and col0 last.

questions.name: Search a 2D Matrix
questions.statement: Given m×n matrix where each row is sorted and each row's first element > previous row's last element, efficiently search for target.
questions.hint: Treat 2D matrix as 1D sorted array. Virtual index mid maps to matrix[mid/n][mid%n].
questions.solution: lo=0, hi=m*n-1. While lo<=hi: mid=(lo+hi)/2; val=matrix[mid/n][mid%n]. If val==target return true. If val<target lo=mid+1 else hi=mid-1. Return false.

questions.name: Range Minimum Query
questions.statement: Given an integer array, handle multiple range minimum queries: find the minimum element between indices left and right (inclusive).
questions.hint: Build segment tree where each node stores minimum of its range.
questions.solution: Build: each leaf = nums[i]; internal node = min(children). Query: if range fully inside return node val; if fully outside return INF; else return min of query on both children.

questions.name: Design Circular Queue
questions.statement: Design a circular queue of size k with enQueue, deQueue, Front, Rear, isEmpty, isFull operations.
questions.hint: Use array of size k with front and rear pointers. Use modular arithmetic for wrap-around.
questions.solution: Array[k], front=0, rear=-1, count=0. enQueue: if full return false; rear=(rear+1)%k; array[rear]=val; count++. deQueue: if empty return false; front=(front+1)%k; count--.

questions.name: LCA of Binary Tree
questions.statement: Given a binary tree (not BST) and two nodes p and q, find the Lowest Common Ancestor. The LCA is the deepest node that has both p and q as descendants.
questions.hint: If root is p or q return root. Recurse left and right. If both non-null, root is LCA.
questions.solution: If root==null or root==p or root==q return root. left=lca(left,p,q); right=lca(right,p,q). If both non-null return root. Return whichever is non-null.
